title: orchestration workflow schema (the steps model)
description: Defines the canonical shape of a workflow.yaml. The single root node is `workflow`; its `steps` constitute the flow. Steps form a flow with three shapes, all expressed through `after` conditions: FORK = sibling steps sharing one `after`; MERGE = a step with several `after`s; ITERATION/LOOP = the acyclic step body re-executed per item at runtime — the `after` DAG stays acyclic (no graph cycles), the run log tolerates repeated step executions (replay = latest-line-wins, ordering uses first-occurrence), and the loop is bounded by a `consistency`/completion condition. Each step's obligations are ONE flat `conditions` list, isomorphic to the run log's conditions[]: each condition has `kind` (precondition / postcondition / invariant), `type` (a judgment category authority/content/clarification/challenge/consistency/transition/state, or a structural dependency after/input/output), `expression` (`cel` harness-evaluated / `instruction` resolved structurally / `ref` structurally resolved), and `value`. Structural refs (after/input/output) are checked STRUCTURALLY, never via CEL; after/input are preconditions, output a postcondition. There is no per-workflow Python. Mediation is an implicit harness function — there is no `facilitate` step kind; participant→participant `after` edges are legal and the harness sequences them. See the harness README Logging section.

identifier.description: A lowercase, hyphen/underscore slug (workflow id, step id).

conditionId.description: A snake_case predicate id, unique within its step; it appears in the run log's conditions[] entries.

condition.description: One entry in a step's flat `conditions` list. Four orthogonal attributes: `kind` = WHEN it holds (precondition / postcondition / invariant); `type` = WHAT it is about — a judgment category (authority / content / clarification / challenge / consistency / transition / state) or a structural dependency (`after` a predecessor step, `input` a consumed artifact, `output` a produced artifact); `expression` = the FORM of `value` (`cel` a harness-evaluated predicate, `instruction` a relative URI to a `.instructions.md` obligation file the harness resolves structurally, `ref` a path/step-id the harness resolves structurally); `value` = the body itself. Coherence is enforced by the schema: every condition carries an `id`; structural types (after/input/output) use `expression: ref`; `after`/`input` are preconditions and `output` a postcondition; a precondition/postcondition judgment uses `expression: cel` and an invariant uses `expression: instruction`. Isomorphic to the run log's conditions[].

kind.description: WHEN the condition holds: precondition (before the step acts), postcondition (after), invariant (throughout).

type.description: WHAT the condition is about: a judgment category (authority/content/clarification/challenge/consistency/transition/state) or a structural dependency — `after` (a predecessor step that must be logged complete), `input` (an artifact consumed before the step), `output` (an artifact produced after the step).

id.description: Stable id, required on EVERY condition — the handle in the run log, findings, and check-step. Structural refs use a generated id (e.g. after_<step>); for `instruction` it keys the obligation file.

expression.description: The form of `value`: `cel` (a CEL predicate the harness evaluates against the read-only fact set — every precondition/postcondition); `instruction` (a relative URI to a `.instructions.md` obligation file the harness resolves structurally — every invariant; the agent follows it); `ref` (a repo path / {unit_id}/{product} template / predecessor step id — structural after/input/output).

value.description: The body: a CEL expression (e.g. `status == "funnel"`, `has(unit.wsjf)`, `gate_packet_ok`), a natural-language judgment, or a ref (e.g. `portfolio/epics/{unit_id}.md`, or a predecessor step id when type: after).

step.description: One structurant step (one actor's turn). Its obligations are ONE flat `conditions` list — each entry a {kind, type, expression, value, id} record (structural refs + cel/instruction judgments), isomorphic to the run log's conditions[]. Exactly one `actor` per step (one actor per turn): a turn taken by several participants is several forked steps, not one. Conventional authoring order groups conditions by kind: preconditions (after, input, judgment preconditions), invariants, postconditions (output, judgment postconditions). NOTE: a step's own `kind` (author/challenge/delegate/render/gate/commit/verify/direct/…) is the step's nature and is unrelated to a condition's `kind` (precondition/postcondition/invariant).

delegates_to.description: For kind: delegate — the sub-workflow this step runs (path under workflows/).

skills.description: The ordered skill ids the dispatched agent loads for THIS step (per-step, not per-workflow). The harness injects them as deterministic context at sessionStart (inject-skills); a sub-orchestration is a step, so its procedure skill rides here.

role.description: Routing metadata: the bench role used for the role-default tier floor (ModelRouter.role_default). Optional — when absent the harness derives it from `actor` (leading `@` stripped).

risk.description: Routing metadata: risk class. critical -> tier-high floor; medium -> tier-balanced; low -> role default.

complexity.description: Routing metadata: complexity class. complex -> tier-high floor; involved -> tier-balanced; simple -> no raise.

tags.description: Routing metadata: capability tags scored by ModelRouter to resolve the concrete model.

config.description: Routing metadata: the config profile dispatched with the step.

output.description: The single artifact KIND this step produces or updates (effect-determinism, invariant 15). Resolves to a real, owned, typed artifact via the artifact catalog + ACL. The structural `type: output` condition still carries the path ref; this names the kind.

conditions.description: The step's flat obligations list — {kind, type, expression, value} records. Isomorphic to the run log's conditions[].

workflow.description: The single root node. Carries the workflow header (id, rank, kind/parent or drives/fsm) plus the `steps` that constitute it. Root workflows declare drives/fsm; sub-workflows declare kind/parent.

version.description: Workflow schema version. 2 = the steps model.

skills.description: DEPRECATED (transitional) — skills are per STEP, not per workflow (see definitions.step.skills). Still tolerated here while the workflows are migrated; Phase 4 moves each skill onto the step that loads it and removes this. The root is harness-driven, so it has no workflow-level skill.

steps.description: The structurant steps constituting this workflow (one actor per step; fork/merge/loop via `after`).
